# Numbers Chapter 14 — The EMET Bible (Literal)

**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), pp. 164–166; Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), pp. 122–123 (LXX) | **Source Images:** `NUM_014_page_001.jpg`, `NUM_014_page_002.jpg`, `NUM_014_page_003.jpg`; `NUM_014_vaticanus_page_001.jpg`, `NUM_014_vaticanus_page_002.jpg`

---

> **MANUSCRIPT OBSERVATION — THE GREAT REBELLION AND ITS CONSEQUENCES:** Numbers 14 is the climax of the spy narrative begun in chapter 13 and the theological hinge of the entire book. Here the wilderness generation seals its fate. The chapter divides into five movements: the people's weeping and rebellion (vv. 1–4), the intercession of Joshua, Caleb, Moses, and Aaron (vv. 5–10), YHWH's proposed annihilation and Moses' intercession (vv. 11–19), YHWH's pardon with judgment — the generation shall die in the wilderness (vv. 20–38), and the presumptuous attempt to invade Canaan without YHWH's sanction (vv. 39–45). On the Leningrad Codex the chapter begins on page 164 (folio 84 recto, left column) and extends through pages 165 and 166. The Vaticanus covers this material on pages 122–123. The chapter contains one of the most theologically dense divine speeches in the Torah (vv. 11–35), including the great declaration of YHWH's character in vv. 18–19 — an echo of the Exodus 34:6–7 self-revelation — and the solemn oath formula חַי־אָנִי (khai ani, "as I live"). The LXX follows the MT closely in this chapter with only minor expansions. The Hebrew text preserves several grammatically difficult constructions that this translation retains without smoothing.

---

## The People Weep and Rebel (vv. 1–4)

**1** And-lifted-up [va-tissa] all [kol] the-congregation [ha-edah] and-they-gave [va-yittenu] their-voice [et-qolam], and-wept [va-yivku] the-people [ha-am] in-the-night [ba-laylah] the-that [ha-hu].

> **Key terms:** וַתִּשָּׂא כָּל־הָעֵדָה וַיִּתְּנוּ אֶת־קוֹלָם (va-tissa kol ha-edah va-yittenu et qolam) = "and all the congregation lifted up and they gave their voice" — the verb נשׂא (nasa, "to lift up") has no stated object; the implied object is "their voice" which follows in the next clause. The idiom נתן קול (natan qol, "to give voice") means to cry out loudly. The singular feminine verb וַתִּשָּׂא with the collective noun עֵדָה (edah, "congregation") shifts to the masculine plural וַיִּתְּנוּ, a common Hebrew construction shift. בַּלַּיְלָה הַהוּא (ba-laylah ha-hu) = "in that night" — the article plus demonstrative specifies the night the spies returned. **LXX:** καὶ ἀναλαβοῦσα πᾶσα ἡ συναγωγὴ ἔδωκεν φωνήν, καὶ ἔκλαιεν ὁ λαὸς ὅλην τὴν νύκτα ἐκείνην ("and all the congregation, taking up, gave voice, and the people wept all that night") — the LXX adds ὅλην ("all/whole") to "that night," an intensifying expansion not in the MT.

**2** And-murmured [va-yillonu] against [al] Moses [Mosheh] and-against [ve-al] Aaron [Aharon] all [kol] the-Sons of [benei] Israel [Yisra'el], and-said [va-yomru] unto-them [alehem] all [kol] the-congregation [ha-edah], "Would-that [lu] we-had-died [matnu] in-the-land of [be-erets] Egypt [Mitsrayim]! Or [o] in-the-wilderness [ba-midbar] the-this [ha-zeh] would-that [lu] we-had-died [matnu]!"

> **Key terms:** וַיִּלֹּנוּ (va-yillonu) = "and they murmured" — from לון (lun, "to murmur, grumble"), the characteristic verb of Israel's wilderness complaints (cf. Exod 15:24; 16:2; 17:3; Num 16:11; 17:6). לוּ מַתְנוּ (lu matnu) = "would that we had died" — the particle לוּ (lu) introduces a contrary-to-fact wish (an irreal conditional), and מַתְנוּ (matnu) is the first person plural qal perfect of מות (mut, "to die"). The wish is bitterly ironic: they will indeed die in the wilderness (v. 29), exactly as they wished. **LXX:** καὶ διεγόγγυζον ἐπὶ Μωυσῆν καὶ Ααρων πάντες οἱ υἱοὶ Ισραηλ ("and all the sons of Israel grumbled against Moses and Aaron").

**3** "And-why [ve-lamah] [does] YHWH bring [mevi] us [otanu] unto [el] the-land [ha-arets] the-this [ha-zot] to-fall [lin-pol] by-the-sword [ba-kherev]? Our-wives [nasheinu] and-our-little-ones [ve-tappeinu] will-become [yihyu] a-plunder [la-vaz]. Is-[it]-not [ha-lo] good [tov] for-us [lanu] to-return [shuv] to-Egypt [Mitsraymah]?"

> **Key terms:** וְלָמָה יְהוָה מֵבִיא אֹתָנוּ (ve-lamah YHWH mevi otanu) = "and why is YHWH bringing us" — the participle מֵבִיא (mevi, "bringing") conveys ongoing action, as if YHWH is in the very act of leading them to slaughter. לִנְפֹּל בַּחֶרֶב (lin-pol ba-kherev) = "to fall by the sword" — the infinitive construct of נפל (nafal, "to fall") with the preposition בְּ instrumental. טַפֵּנוּ (tappeinu) = "our little ones" — from טַף (taf), referring to small children, toddlers. לָבַז (la-vaz) = "for a plunder/prey" — the noun בַּז (baz, "plunder, spoil") with the preposition לְ of result. The irony deepens: it is precisely the little ones whom YHWH will bring into the land (v. 31). הֲלוֹא טוֹב לָנוּ שׁוּב מִצְרָיְמָה (ha-lo tov lanu shuv Mitsraymah) = "is it not better for us to return to Egypt?" — the interrogative הֲ with לֹא creates a rhetorical question expecting assent. מִצְרַיְמָה (Mitsraymah) = "Egypt-ward" — the he-locale suffix indicates direction of movement. **LXX:** αἱ γυναῖκες ἡμῶν καὶ τὰ παιδία ἡμῶν ἔσονται εἰς διαρπαγήν ("our wives and our children will be for plunder").

**4** And-they-said [va-yomru] each [ish] unto [el] his-brother [akhiv], "Let-us-appoint [nitnah] a-head [rosh] and-let-us-return [ve-nashuvah] to-Egypt [Mitsraymah]."

> **Key terms:** נִתְּנָה רֹאשׁ (nitnah rosh) = "let us give/appoint a head" — the cohortative of נתן (natan, "to give"), with רֹאשׁ (rosh, "head/leader"). This is the most radical statement in the rebellion: they propose to replace Moses with a new leader to take them back to Egypt — an effective reversal of the exodus. The parallel in Nehemiah 9:17 explicitly calls this "appointing a head to return to their slavery." **LXX:** δῶμεν ἀρχηγὸν καὶ ἀποστρέψωμεν εἰς Αἴγυπτον ("let us give a leader and let us turn back to Egypt").

---

## Moses, Aaron, Joshua, and Caleb Respond (vv. 5–10)

**5** And-fell [va-yippol] Moses [Mosheh] and-Aaron [ve-Aharon] upon [al] their-faces [peneihem] before [lif-nei] all [kol] the-assembly of [qehal] the-congregation of [adat] the-Sons of [benei] Israel [Yisra'el].

> **Key terms:** וַיִּפֹּל מֹשֶׁה וְאַהֲרֹן עַל־פְּנֵיהֶם (va-yippol Mosheh ve-Aharon al peneihem) = "and Moses and Aaron fell upon their faces" — the singular verb וַיִּפֹּל with the compound subject is typical Hebrew syntax where the verb agrees with the first (nearest) subject. Falling on one's face is an act of extreme distress or supplication (cf. Gen 17:3; Num 16:4, 22; 20:6). קְהַל עֲדַת בְּנֵי יִשְׂרָאֵל (qehal adat benei Yisra'el) = "the assembly of the congregation of the Sons of Israel" — a double construct chain combining both terms for the gathered community. **LXX:** καὶ ἔπεσεν Μωυσῆς καὶ Ααρων ἐπὶ πρόσωπον ἐναντίον πάσης συναγωγῆς υἱῶν Ισραηλ ("and Moses and Aaron fell upon [their] face before all the congregation of the sons of Israel").

**6** And-Joshua [vi-Yehoshua] son of [bin] Nun [Nun] and-Caleb [ve-Khalev] son of [ben] Jephunneh [Yefunneh], from [min] those-who-spied-out [ha-tarim] the-land [ha-arets], tore [qare'u] their-garments [bigdeihem].

> **Key terms:** הַתָּרִים אֶת־הָאָרֶץ (ha-tarim et ha-arets) = "those who spied out the land" — the qal participle of תור (tur, "to spy out, explore") with the article functioning as a relative clause. קָרְעוּ בִּגְדֵיהֶם (qare'u bigdeihem) = "they tore their garments" — the qal perfect of קרע (qara, "to tear, rend"). Tearing garments is the conventional sign of grief, mourning, or horror at blasphemy (cf. Gen 37:29, 34; 2 Kgs 18:37; 19:1). **LXX:** Ἰησοῦς ὁ τοῦ Ναυη καὶ Χαλεβ ὁ τοῦ Ιεφοννη... διέρρηξαν τὰ ἱμάτια αὐτῶν ("Joshua the [son] of Naue and Chaleb the [son] of Iephonne... tore their garments").

**7** And-they-spoke [va-yomru] unto [el] all [kol] the-congregation of [adat] the-Sons of [benei] Israel [Yisra'el], saying [le'mor], "The-land [ha-arets] which [asher] we-passed-through [avarnu] in-it [vah] to-spy-it-out [la-tur otah] — good [tovah] the-land [ha-arets], exceedingly [me'od], exceedingly [me'od]."

> **Key terms:** טוֹבָה הָאָרֶץ מְאֹד מְאֹד (tovah ha-arets me'od me'od) = "good is the land, exceedingly exceedingly" — the doubling of מְאֹד (me'od, "very, exceedingly") creates a superlative emphasis. The predicate adjective טוֹבָה (tovah, "good") precedes the subject for emphasis. **LXX:** ἡ γῆ, ἣν κατεσκεψάμεθα αὐτήν, ἀγαθὴ ἡ γῆ σφόδρα σφόδρα ("the land which we spied out — good is the land, exceedingly exceedingly").

**8** "If [im] YHWH [is] delighting [khafets] in-us [banu], and-he-will-bring-us [ve-hevi otanu] unto [el] the-land [ha-arets] the-this [ha-zot] and-he-will-give-it [u-netanah] to-us [lanu] — a-land [erets] which [asher] she [hi] [is] flowing [zavat] [with] milk [khalav] and-honey [u-devash]."

> **Key terms:** אִם־חָפֵץ בָּנוּ יְהוָה (im khafets banu YHWH) = "if YHWH delights in us" — the particle אִם (im, "if") introduces a real condition (not contrary-to-fact). חָפֵץ (khafets) = "delighting, taking pleasure in" — a qal participle expressing YHWH's ongoing disposition. זָבַת חָלָב וּדְבָשׁ (zavat khalav u-devash) = "flowing [with] milk and honey" — the feminine qal participle of זוב (zuv, "to flow") in construct with the nouns. This is the stock phrase for the land's abundance (cf. Exod 3:8, 17; 13:5; 33:3; Lev 20:24). **LXX:** εἰ αἱρετίζει ἡμᾶς κύριος, εἰσάξει ἡμᾶς εἰς τὴν γῆν ταύτην ("if the Lord chooses us, he will bring us into this land").

**9** "Only [akh] against-YHWH [ba-YHWH] do-not [al] rebel [timrodu], and-you [ve-attem] — do-not [al] fear [tire'u] the-people of [et-am] the-land [ha-arets], for [ki] our-bread [lakhmenu] they [hem] [are]. Their-shade [tsilam] has-departed [sar] from-upon-them [me-alehem], and-YHWH [is] with-us [ittanu]. Do-not [al] fear-them [tire'um]."

> **Key terms:** אַךְ בַּיהוָה אַל־תִּמְרֹדוּ (akh ba-YHWH al timrodu) = "only, against YHWH do not rebel" — אַךְ (akh) is a restrictive particle ("only, however") that pivots from encouragement to warning. תִּמְרֹדוּ (timrodu) is the qal imperfect of מרד (marad, "to rebel"), the same verb used of political insurrection. לַחְמֵנוּ הֵם (lakhmenu hem) = "our bread they are" — a metaphor meaning "we will consume/devour them easily" (cf. the idiom "to eat up" an enemy). צִלָּם סָר מֵעֲלֵיהֶם (tsilam sar me-alehem) = "their shade has departed from upon them" — צֵל (tsel, "shade, shadow") here signifies divine protection or the protective spirit of a people. The verb סָר (sar) is the qal perfect of סור (sur, "to turn aside, depart"). Their gods/protection have abandoned them. **LXX:** ἀλλὰ ἀπὸ τοῦ κυρίου μὴ ἀποστάται γίνεσθε· ὑμεῖς δὲ μὴ φοβηθῆτε τὸν λαὸν τῆς γῆς, ὅτι κατάβρωμα ἡμῖν ἐστιν ("but do not become rebels from the Lord; and do not fear the people of the land, for they are food for us") — the LXX renders לַחְמֵנוּ as κατάβρωμα ("food/fodder"), making the metaphor more explicit. The LXX adds ἀφέστηκεν ὁ καιρὸς ἀπ᾽ αὐτῶν ("the time/opportunity has departed from them"), apparently reading צִלָּם as related to a different sense.

**10** And-said [va-yomru] all [kol] the-congregation [ha-edah] to-stone [lir-gom] them [otam] with-stones [ba-avanim]. And-the-glory of [u-khevod] YHWH appeared [nir'ah] in-the-tent of [be-ohel] meeting [mo'ed] unto [el] all [kol] the-Sons of [benei] Israel [Yisra'el].

> **Key terms:** לִרְגּוֹם אֹתָם בָּאֲבָנִים (lir-gom otam ba-avanim) = "to stone them with stones" — the infinitive construct of רגם (ragam, "to stone") with the cognate noun אֲבָנִים (avanim, "stones"), a redundancy that emphasizes the violence of the intent. The congregation threatens to execute the four faithful men (Moses, Aaron, Joshua, Caleb). וּכְבוֹד יְהוָה נִרְאָה (u-khevod YHWH nir'ah) = "and the glory of YHWH appeared" — the niphal perfect of ראה (ra'ah, "to see"); the כָּבוֹד (kavod, "glory, weight, honor") is the visible manifestation of YHWH's presence (cf. Exod 16:10; 24:16–17; 40:34–35). The appearance halts the stoning — YHWH himself intervenes at the moment of maximum crisis. **LXX:** καὶ εἶπεν πᾶσα ἡ συναγωγὴ καταλιθοβολῆσαι αὐτοὺς ἐν λίθοις· καὶ ἡ δόξα κυρίου ὤφθη ἐν νεφέλῃ ἐπὶ τῆς σκηνῆς τοῦ μαρτυρίου ("and all the congregation said to stone them with stones; and the glory of the Lord appeared in [a] cloud upon the tent of witness") — the LXX adds ἐν νεφέλῃ ("in a cloud"), not present in the MT.

---

## YHWH's Wrath and Moses' Intercession (vv. 11–19)

**11** And-said [va-yomer] YHWH unto [el] Moses [Mosheh], "Until-when [ad-anah] will-spurn-me [yena'atsuni] the-people [ha-am] the-this [ha-zeh]? And-until-when [ve-ad-anah] will-they-not-believe [lo ya'aminu] in-me [bi] with-all [be-khol] the-signs [ha-otot] which [asher] I-have-done [asiti] in-their-midst [be-qirbo]?"

> **Key terms:** עַד־אָנָה (ad anah) = "until when? / how long?" — the interrogative adverb of time, expressing YHWH's exasperation. יְנַאֲצֻנִי (yena'atsuni) = "will they spurn/despise me" — the piel imperfect of נאץ (na'ats, "to spurn, treat with contempt, blaspheme"), with the first person singular suffix. This is not mere disobedience but contemptuous rejection. לֹא־יַאֲמִינוּ בִי (lo ya'aminu vi) = "they will not believe in me" — the hiphil imperfect of אמן (aman, "to be firm, reliable"), meaning "to trust, put faith in." The preposition בְּ (be) indicates personal trust directed toward YHWH. הָאֹתוֹת (ha-otot) = "the signs" — referring to the plagues in Egypt and the wilderness miracles. **LXX:** ἕως τίνος παροξύνει με ὁ λαὸς οὗτος; καὶ ἕως τίνος οὐ πιστεύουσίν μοι ἐν πᾶσιν τοῖς σημείοις οἷς ἐποίησα ἐν αὐτοῖς; ("how long will this people provoke me? and how long will they not believe me among all the signs which I did among them?").

**12** "I-will-strike-them [akkenu] with-the-pestilence [ba-dever] and-I-will-dispossess-them [ve-orishenu], and-I-will-make [ve-e'eseh] you [otkha] into-a-nation [le-goy] greater [gadol] and-mightier [ve-atsum] than-them [mimmenu]."

> **Key terms:** אַכֶּנּוּ בַדֶּבֶר (akkenu va-dever) = "I will strike him with the pestilence" — the hiphil imperfect first person singular of נכה (nakah, "to strike") with the third masculine singular suffix (collective). דֶּבֶר (dever) = "pestilence, plague." וְאוֹרִשֶׁנּוּ (ve-orishenu) = "and I will dispossess him" — the hiphil imperfect of ירשׁ (yarash, "to possess/dispossess"), meaning to strip them of their inheritance. This echoes the threat to destroy Israel and start over with Moses, parallel to the golden calf episode (Exod 32:10). **LXX:** πατάξω αὐτοὺς θανάτῳ καὶ ἀπολῶ αὐτούς, καὶ ποιήσω σὲ καὶ τὸν οἶκον τοῦ πατρός σου εἰς ἔθνος μέγα ("I will strike them with death and destroy them, and I will make you and your father's house into a great nation") — the LXX adds "and your father's house," expanding the scope of the new nation.

**13** And-said [va-yomer] Moses [Mosheh] unto [el] YHWH, "And-will-hear [ve-shame'u] Egypt [Mitsrayim] — for [ki] you-brought-up [he'elita] in-your-strength [be-khokha'kha] the-people [ha-am] the-this [ha-zeh] from-their-midst [mi-qirbo] —"

> **Key terms:** וְשָׁמְעוּ מִצְרַיִם (ve-shame'u Mitsrayim) = "and Egypt will hear" — Moses begins his intercession not by defending the people but by appealing to YHWH's reputation among the nations. מִצְרַיִם (Mitsrayim) here is metonymic for "the Egyptians." כִּי־הֶעֱלִיתָ בְכֹחֲךָ (ki he'elita ve-khokha'kha) = "for you brought up by your strength" — the hiphil perfect of עלה (alah, "to go up"), emphasizing YHWH's power in the exodus. **LXX:** καὶ ἀκούσεται Αἴγυπτος, ὅτι ἀνήγαγες ἐν ἰσχύι σου τὸν λαὸν τοῦτον ἐξ αὐτῶν ("and Egypt will hear that you brought up this people from among them by your strength").

**14** "And-they-will-say [ve-amru] unto [el] the-inhabitants of [yoshev] the-land [ha-arets] the-this [ha-zot]: they-have-heard [shame'u] that [ki] you [attah], YHWH, [are] in-the-midst of [be-qerev] the-people [ha-am] the-this [ha-zeh], who [asher] eye [ayin] to [be] eye [ayin] is-seen [nir'ah] — you [attah], YHWH — and-your-cloud [va-anankha] [is] standing [omed] over-them [alehem], and-in-a-pillar of [u-ve-ammud] cloud [anan] you [attah] [are] walking [holekh] before-them [lifneihem] by-day [yomam] and-in-a-pillar of [u-ve-ammud] fire [esh] by-night [laylah]."

> **Key terms:** עַיִן בְּעַיִן נִרְאָה אַתָּה יְהוָה (ayin be-ayin nir'ah attah YHWH) = "eye to eye is seen — you, YHWH" — this striking phrase means YHWH is seen face-to-face, directly, without mediation. The niphal of ראה with the idiom עַיִן בְּעַיִן ("eye in/to eye") conveys intimate, visible divine presence. וַעֲנָנְךָ עֹמֵד עֲלֵהֶם (va-anankha omed alehem) = "and your cloud is standing over them" — the cloud is YHWH's personal possession (עֲנָנְךָ, "your cloud"). וּבְעַמֻּד עָנָן... וּבְעַמֻּד אֵשׁ (u-ve-ammud anan... u-ve-ammud esh) = "and in a pillar of cloud... and in a pillar of fire" — the two manifestations of YHWH's leading presence (cf. Exod 13:21–22). Moses argues: the nations know YHWH is present with Israel; destroying them would be seen as inability, not judgment. **LXX:** ὅτι σὺ κύριος ἐν τῷ λαῷ τούτῳ, ὅστις ὀφθαλμοῖς κατ᾽ ὀφθαλμοὺς ὀπτάζῃ, κύριε ("that you, Lord, [are] in this people, who [are] seen eye to eye, Lord").

**15** "And-you-will-put-to-death [ve-hemattah] the-people [ha-am] the-this [ha-zeh] as-one [ke-ish] man [ekhad], and-will-say [ve-amar] the-nations [ha-goyim] who [asher] have-heard [shame'u] your-report [shim'akha], saying [le'mor]:"

> **Key terms:** וְהֵמַתָּה אֶת־הָעָם הַזֶּה כְּאִישׁ אֶחָד (ve-hemattah et ha-am ha-zeh ke-ish ekhad) = "and you will kill this people as one man" — the hiphil perfect (with waw-consecutive) of מות (mut, "to die"), meaning "you will cause to die / you will put to death." כְּאִישׁ אֶחָד (ke-ish ekhad) = "as one man" — meaning all at once, in a single stroke. **LXX:** καὶ ἀποκτενεῖς τὸν λαὸν τοῦτον ὡσεὶ ἄνθρωπον ἕνα ("and you will kill this people as one man").

**16** "From-the-inability of [mi-bilti yekhol-et] YHWH to-bring [le-havi] the-people [ha-am] the-this [ha-zeh] unto [el] the-land [ha-arets] which [asher] he-swore [nishba] to-them [lahem], and-he-slaughtered-them [va-yishkhatem] in-the-wilderness [ba-midbar]."

> **Key terms:** מִבִּלְתִּי יְכֹלֶת יְהוָה (mi-bilti yekhol-et YHWH) = "from the inability of YHWH" — this is a compound preposition: מִן (min, "from") + בִּלְתִּי (bilti, "not/without") + יְכֹלֶת (yekhol-et, "ability/power," the infinitive construct of יכל used as a noun). Moses puts these words in the mouths of the nations: they would interpret Israel's destruction as YHWH's impotence, not his justice. וַיִּשְׁחָטֵם (va-yishkhatem) = "and he slaughtered them" — the qal wayyiqtol of שׁחט (shakhat, "to slaughter"), a verb typically used for sacrificial slaughter of animals, making the image brutally vivid. **LXX:** παρὰ τὸ μὴ δύνασθαι κύριον εἰσαγαγεῖν τὸν λαὸν τοῦτον εἰς τὴν γῆν ἣν ὤμοσεν αὐτοῖς, κατέστρωσεν αὐτοὺς ἐν τῇ ἐρήμῳ ("because the Lord was not able to bring this people into the land which he swore to them, he struck them down in the wilderness").

**17** "And-now [ve-attah], let-be-great [yigdal], please [na], the-power of [ko'akh] my-Lord [Adonai], as [ka-asher] you-spoke [dibbarta], saying [le'mor]:"

> **Key terms:** יִגְדַּל־נָא כֹּחַ אֲדֹנָי (yigdal na ko'akh Adonai) = "let the power of my Lord be great, please" — the jussive of גדל (gadal, "to be great") with the particle of entreaty נָא (na, "please, I pray"). Moses asks YHWH to demonstrate his greatness not through destruction but through forbearance. כַּאֲשֶׁר דִּבַּרְתָּ (ka-asher dibbarta) = "as you spoke" — referring to YHWH's self-revelation of his own character, which Moses is about to quote. **LXX:** καὶ νῦν ὑψωθήτω ἡ ἰσχύς σου, κύριε, ὃν τρόπον εἶπας λέγων ("and now let your strength be exalted, Lord, in the way you said, saying").

**18** "YHWH, slow [erekh] of-nostrils [appayim] and-great of [ve-rav] steadfast-love [khesed], bearing [nosei] iniquity [avon] and-transgression [va-fasha], and-acquitting [ve-naqqeh] — he-will-not [lo] acquit [yenaqqeh], visiting [poqed] the-iniquity of [avon] fathers [avot] upon [al] sons [banim], upon [al] the-third-[generation] [shilleshim] and-upon [ve-al] the-fourth-[generation] [ribbe'im]."

> **Key terms:** יְהוָה אֶרֶךְ אַפַּיִם וְרַב־חֶסֶד (YHWH erekh appayim ve-rav khesed) — this is the great divine self-description first revealed at Sinai (Exod 34:6–7). אֶרֶךְ אַפַּיִם (erekh appayim) = literally "long of nostrils" — since אַף (af) means both "nostril" and "anger" (anger being expressed by heavy breathing through the nostrils), the idiom means "slow to anger." וְרַב־חֶסֶד (ve-rav khesed) = "and great of steadfast-love/loyalty" — חֶסֶד (khesed) is covenantal faithfulness, loyal love, mercy. נֹשֵׂא עָוֺן וָפָשַׁע (nosei avon va-fasha) = "bearing iniquity and transgression" — the qal participle of נשׂא (nasa, "to bear, carry, lift up") meaning to bear away, i.e., to forgive. וְנַקֵּה לֹא יְנַקֶּה (ve-naqqeh lo yenaqqeh) = "and acquitting, he will not acquit" — the infinitive absolute + finite verb construction (piel of נקה, naqah, "to be clean, innocent") creates emphasis. The meaning is paradoxical: YHWH forgives, yet does not leave guilt entirely unpunished. פֹּקֵד עֲוֺן אָבוֹת עַל־בָּנִים (poqed avon avot al banim) = "visiting the iniquity of fathers upon sons" — the qal participle of פקד (paqad, "to visit, attend to, reckon"). Note: compared to Exodus 34:6–7, the Numbers version omits "compassionate and gracious" (רַחוּם וְחַנּוּן) and "and truth" (וֶאֱמֶת) and "keeping steadfast-love for thousands" — Moses selectively quotes the attributes most relevant to his plea. **LXX:** κύριος μακρόθυμος καὶ πολυέλεος καὶ ἀληθινός, ἀφαιρῶν ἀνομίας καὶ ἀδικίας καὶ ἁμαρτίας ("Lord, long-suffering and very merciful and true, taking away lawlessness and unrighteousness and sins") — the LXX adds καὶ ἀληθινός ("and true"), which the MT here omits.

**19** "Forgive [selakh], please [na], the-iniquity of [la-avon] the-people [ha-am] the-this [ha-zeh] according-to-the-greatness of [ke-godel] your-steadfast-love [khasdekha], and-as [ve-ka-asher] you-have-borne [nasatah] the-people [la-am] the-this [ha-zeh] from-Egypt [mi-Mitsrayim] and-until [ve-ad] here [hennah]."

> **Key terms:** סְלַח־נָא (selakh na) = "forgive, please" — the qal imperative of סלח (salakh, "to forgive, pardon"), a verb used exclusively of divine forgiveness in the Hebrew Bible. כְּגֹדֶל חַסְדֶּךָ (ke-godel khasdekha) = "according to the greatness of your steadfast-love" — Moses grounds his plea in YHWH's own character, not in any merit of the people. וְכַאֲשֶׁר נָשָׂאתָה לָעָם הַזֶּה מִמִּצְרַיִם וְעַד־הֵנָּה (ve-ka-asher nasatah la-am ha-zeh mi-Mitsrayim ve-ad hennah) = "and as you have borne/forgiven this people from Egypt and until here" — נָשָׂאתָה (nasatah) is the second person masculine singular qal perfect of נשׂא (nasa) with the paragogic ה. Moses invokes YHWH's track record of forbearance since the exodus. **LXX:** ἄφες τὴν ἁμαρτίαν τῷ λαῷ τούτῳ κατὰ τὸ μέγα ἔλεός σου, καθάπερ ἵλεως αὐτοῖς ἐγένου ἀπ᾽ Αἰγύπτου ἕως τοῦ νῦν ("forgive the sin of this people according to your great mercy, just as you were gracious to them from Egypt until now").

---

## YHWH's Pardon and Judgment (vv. 20–38)

**20** And-said [va-yomer] YHWH, "I-have-forgiven [salakhti] according-to-your-word [ki-dvarekha]."

> **Key terms:** סָלַחְתִּי כִּדְבָרֶךָ (salakhti ki-dvarekha) = "I have forgiven according to your word" — the qal perfect first person singular of סלח (salakh). The brevity of this divine response is stunning after Moses' lengthy intercession. YHWH forgives — but what follows (vv. 21–35) shows that forgiveness does not mean removal of all consequences. כִּדְבָרֶךָ (ki-dvarekha) = "according to your word" — YHWH credits Moses' intercession as the ground of pardon. **LXX:** ἵλεως αὐτοῖς εἰμι κατὰ τὸ ῥῆμά σου ("I am gracious to them according to your word").

**21** "And-yet [ve-ulam], [as] I-live [khai ani], and-shall-be-filled [ve-yimmale] the-glory of [khevod] YHWH [with] all [et-kol] the-earth [ha-arets] —"

> **Key terms:** וְאוּלָם חַי־אָנִי (ve-ulam khai ani) = "and yet, as I live" — the adversative particle וְאוּלָם (ve-ulam, "but, however, yet") introduces the counter-statement. חַי־אָנִי (khai ani) = "I live" / "as I live" — a divine oath formula; YHWH swears by his own life since there is no one greater to swear by (cf. Deut 32:40; Isa 49:18; Jer 22:24; Ezek 5:11). וְיִמָּלֵא כְבוֹד־יְהוָה אֶת־כָּל־הָאָרֶץ (ve-yimmale khevod YHWH et kol ha-arets) = "and the glory of YHWH shall fill all the earth" — the niphal imperfect of מלא (male, "to fill"). This is both an assertion and a purpose statement: YHWH's glory will fill the earth precisely through the way he handles this crisis. **LXX:** ἀλλὰ ζῶ ἐγὼ καὶ ζῶν τὸ ὄνομά μου καὶ ἐμπλήσει ἡ δόξα κυρίου πᾶσαν τὴν γῆν ("but I live, and living [is] my name, and the glory of [the] Lord shall fill all the earth") — the LXX adds "and living is my name."

**22** "For [ki] all [kol] the-men [ha-anashim] the-seeing [ha-ro'im] my-glory [et-khevodi] and-my-signs [ve-et-ototai] which [asher] I-did [asiti] in-Egypt [be-Mitsrayim] and-in-the-wilderness [u-va-midbar], and-they-tested [va-yenassu] me [oti] this [zeh] ten [eser] times [pe'amim], and-they-have-not-listened [ve-lo shame'u] to-my-voice [be-qoli] —"

> **Key terms:** וַיְנַסּוּ אֹתִי זֶה עֶשֶׂר פְּעָמִים (va-yenassu oti zeh eser pe'amim) = "and they tested me this ten times" — the piel wayyiqtol of נסה (nasah, "to test, try"). The demonstrative זֶה (zeh, "this") functions adverbially: "these ten times" or "now ten times." The number ten may be literal (Rabbinic tradition identifies ten specific tests) or may be a round number for "repeatedly." **LXX:** καὶ ἐπείρασάν με τοῦτο δέκατον ("and they tested me this tenth [time]") — the LXX renders "this tenth" (δέκατον, ordinal) rather than "ten times" (cardinal), implying this is the tenth and final test.

**23** "If [im] they-shall-see [yir'u] the-land [et-ha-arets] which [asher] I-swore [nishbati] to-their-fathers [la-avotam]! And-all [ve-khol] those-who-spurn-me [mena'atsai] — they-shall-not [lo] see-it [yir'uha]."

> **Key terms:** אִם־יִרְאוּ (im yir'u) = "if they shall see" — this is an oath formula where אִם (im, "if") introduces a self-imprecation: "if they shall see [the land, may I not be God]" — effectively meaning "they shall certainly NOT see." This is the strongest possible denial, an oath by YHWH himself. מְנַאֲצַי (mena'atsai) = "those who spurn me" — the piel participle of נאץ (na'ats, "to spurn, contemn") with the first person singular suffix, forming a substantive: "my spurners." **LXX:** ἦ μὴν οὐκ ὄψονται τὴν γῆν ἣν ὤμοσα τοῖς πατράσιν αὐτῶν ("truly they shall not see the land which I swore to their fathers") — the LXX unpacks the Hebrew oath formula into a direct negative statement.

**24** "And-my-servant [ve-avdi] Caleb [Khalev], because [eqev] there-was [hayetah] a-different [akher-et] spirit [ru'akh] with-him [immo] and-he-followed-fully [va-yemalle] after-me [akharai], and-I-will-bring-him [va-havi'otiv] unto [el] the-land [ha-arets] which [asher] he-went [ba] there [shammah], and-his-seed [ve-zar'o] shall-possess-it [yorishennah]."

> **Key terms:** עַבְדִּי כָלֵב (avdi Khalev) = "my servant Caleb" — the title עֶבֶד (eved, "servant") when applied by YHWH is a term of highest honor (cf. Moses: Num 12:7–8; David: 2 Sam 7:5). עֵקֶב (eqev) = "because/inasmuch as" — literally "heel/consequence," used as a causal conjunction. רוּחַ אַחֶרֶת (ru'akh akheret) = "a different spirit" — not a different divine spirit, but a different disposition/attitude: faithfulness rather than fear. וַיְמַלֵּא אַחֲרָי (va-yemalle akharai) = "and he filled after me" — literally "he made full [in going] after me," the idiom for complete, wholehearted devotion (cf. Deut 1:36; Josh 14:8–9, 14). Note: Joshua is not mentioned here but is implicitly included (cf. v. 30, 38). **LXX:** ὁ δὲ παῖς μου Χαλεβ, ὅτι ἐγενήθη πνεῦμα ἕτερον ἐν αὐτῷ καὶ ἐπηκολούθησέν μοι ("but my servant Chaleb, because there was a different spirit in him and he followed me").

**25** "And-the-Amalekite [ve-ha-Amaleqi] and-the-Canaanite [ve-ha-Kena'ani] [are] dwelling [yoshev] in-the-valley [ba-emeq]. Tomorrow [makhar] turn [penu] and-journey [u-se'u] for-yourselves [lakhem] [into] the-wilderness [ha-midbarah] [by] the-way of [derekh] the-Sea of [Yam] Reeds [Suf]."

> **Key terms:** וְהָעֲמָלֵקִי וְהַכְּנַעֲנִי יוֹשֵׁב בָּעֵמֶק (ve-ha-Amaleqi ve-ha-Kena'ani yoshev ba-emeq) = "and the Amalekite and the Canaanite are dwelling in the valley" — the singular participle יוֹשֵׁב (yoshev, "dwelling") with the compound subject treats them as a collective unit. This parenthetical statement provides the practical reason for the next command. פְּנוּ וּסְעוּ לָכֶם הַמִּדְבָּרָה (penu u-se'u lakhem ha-midbarah) = "turn and journey for yourselves toward the wilderness" — two imperatives followed by the ethical dative לָכֶם (lakhem, "for yourselves"). The he-locale suffix on הַמִּדְבָּרָה (ha-midbarah) indicates direction. דֶּרֶךְ יַם־סוּף (derekh Yam Suf) = "the way of the Sea of Reeds" — the route leading back south, away from Canaan. This is the immediate reversal of their journey: they must turn around. **LXX:** ὁ Αμαληκ καὶ ὁ Χαναναῖος κατοικοῦσιν ἐν τῇ κοιλάδι· αὔριον ἐπιστράφητε ὑμεῖς καὶ ἀπάρατε εἰς τὴν ἔρημον ὁδὸν θάλασσαν ἐρυθράν ("the Amalek and the Canaanite dwell in the valley; tomorrow turn and depart into the wilderness, the way of the Red Sea").

**26** And-spoke [va-yedabber] YHWH unto [el] Moses [Mosheh] and-unto [ve-el] Aaron [Aharon], saying [le'mor]:

> **Key terms:** This is the second divine speech formula in the chapter, marking a new section of the judgment oracle. YHWH now addresses both Moses and Aaron.

**27** "Until-when [ad matai] [will I bear] the-congregation [la-edah] the-evil [ha-ra'ah] the-this [ha-zot], which [asher] they [hemmah] [are] murmuring [mallinim] against-me [alai]? The-murmurings of [et-telunnot] the-Sons of [benei] Israel [Yisra'el] which [asher] they [hemmah] [are] murmuring [mallinim] against-me [alai] — I-have-heard [shamati]."

> **Key terms:** עַד־מָתַי לָעֵדָה הָרָעָה הַזֹּאת (ad matai la-edah ha-ra'ah ha-zot) = "how long [shall I endure] this evil congregation" — the sentence is elliptical, with the main verb implied. The lamed on לָעֵדָה is a dativus incommodi ("with respect to / concerning"). מַלִּינִם עָלָי (mallinim alai) = "murmuring against me" — the hiphil participle of לון/לין (lun, "to murmur"), here nominalized. אֶת־תְּלֻנּוֹת (et-telunnot) = "the murmurings" — the noun תְּלוּנָה (telunnah, "murmuring, complaint"), a cognate of the verb. שָׁמָעְתִּי (shamati) = "I have heard" — placed emphatically at the end: YHWH has heard everything. **LXX:** ἕως τίνος τὴν συναγωγὴν τὴν πονηρὰν ταύτην; ἃ αὐτοὶ γογγύζουσιν ἐναντίον ἐμοῦ, τὴν γόγγυσιν τῶν υἱῶν Ισραηλ, ἣν ἐγόγγυσαν περὶ ὑμῶν, ἀκήκοα ("how long this evil congregation? what they grumble before me, the grumbling of the sons of Israel, which they grumbled concerning you, I have heard").

**28** "Say [emor] to-them [alehem], '[As] I-live [khai ani] — declares [ne'um] YHWH — if-not [im lo] as [ka-asher] you-spoke [dibbartem] in-my-ears [be-oznai], thus [ken] I-will-do [e'eseh] to-you [lakhem]:'"

> **Key terms:** חַי־אָנִי נְאֻם־יְהוָה (khai ani ne'um YHWH) = "as I live — declares YHWH" — the oath formula repeated from v. 21, now combined with the prophetic speech formula נְאֻם (ne'um, "utterance/declaration of"). אִם־לֹא כַּאֲשֶׁר דִּבַּרְתֶּם בְּאָזְנָי (im lo ka-asher dibbartem be-oznai) = "if not as you spoke in my ears" — the oath idiom אִם לֹא (im lo, "if not") after חַי אָנִי means "surely, certainly." YHWH turns their own words against them: you wished to die in the wilderness (v. 2); so be it. **LXX:** εἰπὸν αὐτοῖς Ζῶ ἐγώ, λέγει κύριος· ἦ μὴν ὃν τρόπον ἐλαλήσατε εἰς τὰ ὦτά μου, οὕτως ποιήσω ὑμῖν ("say to them: I live, says the Lord; truly in the way you spoke in my ears, so I will do to you").

**29** "'In-the-wilderness [ba-midbar] the-this [ha-zeh] shall-fall [yippelu] your-corpses [figreikhem], and-all [ve-khol] your-counted-ones [pequdeikhem] according-to-all [le-khol] your-number [mispar-khem] from-a-son of [mi-ben] twenty [esrim] year[s] [shanah] and-upward [va-ma'lah] who [asher] you-murmured [halinotem] against-me [alai] —'"

> **Key terms:** פִגְרֵיכֶם (figreikhem) = "your corpses" — from פֶּגֶר (feger, "corpse, carcass"), a degrading term for dead bodies (cf. Isa 14:19; 34:3; 66:24). The use of this word rather than the more neutral מֵת (met, "dead one") or גְּוִיָּה (geviyyah, "body") is deliberately harsh. פְּקֻדֵיכֶם (pequdeikhem) = "your counted/mustered ones" — from פקד (paqad, "to number, muster"), referring to the census of Numbers 1–2. לְכָל־מִסְפַּרְכֶם (le-khol mispar-khem) = "according to all your number" — the entire census roll. מִבֶּן עֶשְׂרִים שָׁנָה וָמַעְלָה (mi-ben esrim shanah va-ma'lah) = "from a son of twenty years and upward" — the military census age (cf. Num 1:3). **LXX:** ἐν τῇ ἐρήμῳ ταύτῃ πεσεῖται τὰ κῶλα ὑμῶν ("in this wilderness shall fall your limbs").

**30** "'If [im] you [attem] shall-enter [tavo'u] unto [el] the-land [ha-arets] which [asher] I-lifted [nasati] my-hand [et-yadi] to-cause-you-to-dwell [le-shakkein etkhem] in-it [bah], except [ki im] Caleb [Kalev] son of [ben] Jephunneh [Yefunneh] and-Joshua [vi-Yehoshua] son of [bin] Nun [Nun].'"

> **Key terms:** אֲשֶׁר נָשָׂאתִי אֶת־יָדִי (asher nasati et yadi) = "which I lifted my hand" — the idiom of raising the hand is the gesture of oath-taking (cf. Gen 14:22; Exod 6:8; Ezek 20:5–6, 15, 23, 28, 42). לְשַׁכֵּן אֶתְכֶם בָּהּ (le-shakkein etkhem bah) = "to cause you to dwell in it" — the piel infinitive construct of שׁכן (shakhan, "to dwell"), in the causative sense. כִּי אִם (ki im) = "except, unless" — the exceptive conjunction. Only Caleb and Joshua are exempted from the decree. **LXX:** ὑμεῖς δὲ οὐκ εἰσελεύσεσθε εἰς τὴν γῆν, ἐφ᾽ ἣν ἐξέτεινα τὴν χεῖρά μου κατασκηνῶσαι ὑμᾶς ἐπ᾽ αὐτῆς, ἀλλ᾽ ἢ Χαλεβ υἱὸς Ιεφοννη καὶ Ἰησοῦς ὁ τοῦ Ναυη ("but you shall not enter the land over which I stretched out my hand to settle you upon it, except Chaleb son of Iephonne and Joshua the [son] of Naue").

**31** "'And-your-little-ones [ve-tappekhem] whom [asher] you-said [amartem] a-plunder [la-vaz] they-will-be [yihyeh] — and-I-will-bring [ve-heveti] them [otam], and-they-shall-know [ve-yade'u] the-land [et-ha-arets] which [asher] you-rejected [me'astem] [in]-her [bah].'"

> **Key terms:** וְטַפְּכֶם אֲשֶׁר אֲמַרְתֶּם לָבַז יִהְיֶה (ve-tappekhem asher amartem la-vaz yihyeh) = "and your little ones whom you said would be a plunder" — YHWH seizes their own argument and reverses it: the children they claimed would be taken as spoil will be the ones who inherit the land. מְאַסְתֶּם (me'astem) = "you rejected/despised" — the qal perfect of מאס (ma'as, "to reject, despise, refuse"), a strong verb of contemptuous dismissal. **LXX:** καὶ τὰ παιδία ὑμῶν, ἃ εἴπατε ἐν διαρπαγῇ ἔσεσθαι, εἰσάξω αὐτοὺς εἰς τὴν γῆν, καὶ γνώσονται τὴν γῆν ἣν ὑμεῖς ἀπέστητε ἀπ᾽ αὐτῆς ("and your children, which you said would be for plunder, I will bring them into the land, and they shall know the land from which you turned away").

**32** "'And-your-corpses [u-figreikhem] — you [attem] — shall-fall [yippelu] in-the-wilderness [ba-midbar] the-this [ha-zeh].'"

> **Key terms:** וּפִגְרֵיכֶם אַתֶּם יִפְּלוּ בַּמִּדְבָּר הַזֶּה (u-figreikhem attem yippelu ba-midbar ha-zeh) = "and your corpses — you — shall fall in this wilderness" — the independent pronoun אַתֶּם (attem, "you") is emphatic, drawing a sharp contrast with the children of v. 31: they will enter; but you — your corpses will fall here.

**33** "'And-your-sons [u-vneikhem] shall-be [yihyu] shepherds [ro'im] in-the-wilderness [ba-midbar] forty [arba'im] year[s] [shanah], and-they-shall-bear [ve-nas'u] your-harlotries [et-zenuteikhem] until [ad] the-completion of [tom] your-corpses [figreikhem] in-the-wilderness [ba-midbar].'"

> **Key terms:** רֹעִים (ro'im) = "shepherds" or "wanderers" — the qal participle of רעה (ra'ah, "to shepherd, pasture"), here meaning to wander as nomadic shepherds. אַרְבָּעִים שָׁנָה (arba'im shanah) = "forty years" — the generation-defining sentence. זְנוּתֵיכֶם (zenuteikhem) = "your harlotries/unfaithfulnesses" — from זְנוּת (zenut, "harlotry, fornication"), used metaphorically for Israel's spiritual infidelity to YHWH. The children will bear the consequences of their parents' faithlessness. עַד־תֹּם פִּגְרֵיכֶם (ad tom figreikhem) = "until the completion/finishing of your corpses" — until every last member of the condemned generation has died. **LXX:** οἱ δὲ υἱοὶ ὑμῶν ἔσονται νεμόμενοι ἐν τῇ ἐρήμῳ τεσσαράκοντα ἔτη καὶ ἀνοίσουσιν τὴν πορνείαν ὑμῶν, ἕως ἂν ἀναλωθῇ τὰ κῶλα ὑμῶν ἐν τῇ ἐρήμῳ ("and your sons shall be pasturing in the wilderness forty years and shall bear your fornication, until your limbs are consumed in the wilderness").

**34** "'According-to-the-number of [be-mispar] the-days [ha-yamim] which [asher] you-spied-out [tartem] the-land [et-ha-arets] — forty [arba'im] day[s] [yom] — a-day [yom] for-a-year [la-shanah], a-day [yom] for-a-year [la-shanah], you-shall-bear [tis'u] your-iniquities [et-avonotei-khem] — forty [arba'im] year[s] [shanah] — and-you-shall-know [vi-da'tem] my-opposition [et-tenu'ati].'"

> **Key terms:** יוֹם לַשָּׁנָה יוֹם לַשָּׁנָה (yom la-shanah yom la-shanah) = "a day for a year, a day for a year" — the repetition establishes the correspondence ratio. The forty days of spying (13:25) become forty years of wandering. תְּנוּאָתִי (tenu'ati) = "my opposition" — from the root נוא (nu', "to oppose, hinder, frustrate"), a rare noun occurring only here in the Hebrew Bible. The meaning is debated: "my alienation/estrangement" or "what it means to oppose me" or "the frustration of [my purpose]." The LXX renders it as τὸν θυμόν τῆς ὀργῆς μου ("the fury of my wrath"), interpreting it as YHWH's anger. The Vulgate renders it as ultionem meam ("my vengeance"). **LXX:** κατὰ τὸν ἀριθμὸν τῶν ἡμερῶν, ὅσας κατεσκέψασθε τὴν γῆν, τεσσαράκοντα ἡμέρας, ἡμέραν τοῦ ἐνιαυτοῦ, λήμψεσθε τὰς ἁμαρτίας ὑμῶν τεσσαράκοντα ἔτη καὶ γνώσεσθε τὸν θυμὸν τῆς ὀργῆς μου ("according to the number of the days in which you spied out the land — forty days — a day for a year, you shall receive your sins forty years and shall know the fury of my wrath").

**35** "'I [ani], YHWH, have-spoken [dibbarti]. If [im] not [lo] this [zot] I-will-do [e'eseh] to-all [le-khol] the-congregation [ha-edah] the-evil [ha-ra'ah] the-this [ha-zot], the-ones-who-assembled [ha-no'adim] against-me [alai]: in-the-wilderness [ba-midbar] the-this [ha-zeh] they-shall-be-finished [yittammu], and-there [ve-sham] they-shall-die [yamutu].'"

> **Key terms:** אֲנִי יְהוָה דִּבַּרְתִּי (ani YHWH dibbarti) = "I, YHWH, have spoken" — the self-identification formula sealing the decree as irrevocable. אִם־לֹא זֹאת אֶעֱשֶׂה (im lo zot e'eseh) = "if not this I will do" — another oath structure: "I shall certainly do this." הַנּוֹעָדִים עָלַי (ha-no'adim alai) = "the ones assembled against me" — the niphal participle of יעד (ya'ad, "to appoint, meet, assemble"), here with hostile intent. יִתַּמּוּ וְשָׁם יָמֻתוּ (yittammu ve-sham yamutu) = "they shall be finished, and there they shall die" — two verbs of finality: תמם (tamam, "to be complete, finished, consumed") and מות (mut, "to die"). **LXX:** ἐγὼ κύριος ἐλάλησα· ἦ μὴν οὕτως ποιήσω τῇ συναγωγῇ τῇ πονηρᾷ ταύτῃ τῇ ἐπισυνεσταμένῃ ἐπ᾽ ἐμέ· ἐν τῇ ἐρήμῳ ταύτῃ ἐξαναλωθήσονται καὶ ἐκεῖ ἀποθανοῦνται ("I, the Lord, have spoken; truly so I will do to this evil congregation that has conspired against me; in this wilderness they shall be utterly consumed and there they shall die").

**36** And-the-men [ve-ha-anashim] whom [asher] Moses [Mosheh] sent [shalakh] to-spy-out [la-tur] the-land [et-ha-arets] and-they-returned [va-yashuvu] and-they-caused-to-murmur [va-yallinu] against-him [alav] all [et-kol] the-congregation [ha-edah] by-bringing-out [le-hotsi] an-evil-report [dibbah] against [al] the-land [ha-arets] —

> **Key terms:** וַיַּלִּינוּ עָלָיו אֶת־כָּל־הָעֵדָה (va-yallinu alav et kol ha-edah) = "and they caused to murmur against him all the congregation" — the hiphil wayyiqtol of לון (lun), the causative form: "they made [the congregation] murmur." The spies did not merely report; they incited rebellion. לְהוֹצִיא דִבָּה עַל־הָאָרֶץ (le-hotsi dibbah al ha-arets) = "to bring out an evil report against the land" — דִּבָּה (dibbah) = "evil report, slander, defamation" (cf. Gen 37:2; Num 13:32; Prov 10:18). **LXX:** οἱ ἄνδρες οὓς ἀπέστειλεν Μωυσῆς κατασκέψασθαι τὴν γῆν, καὶ παραγενηθέντες διεγόγγυσαν κατ᾽ αὐτῆς πρὸς τὴν συναγωγὴν ἐξενέγκαι ῥήματα πονηρὰ περὶ τῆς γῆς ("the men whom Moses sent to spy out the land, and having arrived they grumbled against it to the congregation, bringing out evil words concerning the land").

**37** And-died [va-yamutu] the-men [ha-anashim] who-brought-out [motsi'ei] the-evil-report of [dibbat] the-land [ha-arets] — evil [ra'ah] — by-the-plague [ba-maggefah] before [lif-nei] YHWH.

> **Key terms:** מוֹצִאֵי דִּבַּת הָאָרֶץ רָעָה (motsi'ei dibbat ha-arets ra'ah) = "those who brought out the evil report of the land — evil" — the adjective רָעָה (ra'ah, "evil") stands in apposition to דִּבָּה (dibbah), emphasizing the malicious nature of the report. בַּמַּגֵּפָה (ba-maggefah) = "by the plague" — from נגף (nagaf, "to strike, smite"); the noun מַגֵּפָה means "plague, blow, slaughter." The ten unfaithful spies die immediately by divine plague. **LXX:** καὶ ἀπέθανον οἱ ἄνδρες οἱ κατείπαντες κατὰ τῆς γῆς πονηρά, ἐν τῇ πληγῇ ἔναντι κυρίου ("and the men who spoke evil against the land died by the plague before the Lord").

**38** And-Joshua [vi-Yehoshua] son of [bin] Nun [Nun] and-Caleb [ve-Khalev] son of [ben] Jephunneh [Yefunneh] lived [khayu] from [min] the-men [ha-anashim] the-those [ha-hem] the-ones-who-went [ha-holkhim] to-spy-out [la-tur] the-land [et-ha-arets].

> **Key terms:** חָיוּ (khayu) = "they lived" — the qal perfect of חיה (khayah, "to live"), in stark contrast to וַיָּמֻתוּ (va-yamutu, "and they died") in v. 37. The contrast is absolute: the ten died, these two lived. מִן־הָאֲנָשִׁים הָהֵם (min ha-anashim ha-hem) = "from those men" — the preposition מִן (min) here is partitive: "from among." **LXX:** καὶ Ἰησοῦς υἱὸς Ναυη καὶ Χαλεβ υἱὸς Ιεφοννη ἔζησαν ἀπὸ τῶν ἀνδρῶν ἐκείνων τῶν πεπορευμένων κατασκέψασθαι τὴν γῆν ("and Joshua son of Naue and Chaleb son of Iephonne lived from among those men who had gone to spy out the land").

## The Presumptuous Attempt to Invade (vv. 39–45)

**39** And-spoke [va-yedabber] Moses [Mosheh] the-words [ha-devarim] the-these [ha-elleh] unto [el] all [kol] the-Sons of [benei] Israel [Yisra'el], and-mourned [va-yit'abbelu] the-people [ha-am] exceedingly [me'od].

> **Key terms:** וַיִּתְאַבְּלוּ הָעָם מְאֹד (va-yit'abbelu ha-am me'od) = "and the people mourned exceedingly" — the hithpael wayyiqtol of אבל (aval, "to mourn"), a reflexive form indicating deep, self-conscious grief. **LXX:** καὶ ἐπένθησεν ὁ λαὸς σφόδρα ("and the people mourned exceedingly").

**40** And-they-rose-early [va-yashkimu] in-the-morning [ba-boqer] and-they-went-up [va-ya'alu] unto [el] the-top of [rosh] the-mountain [ha-har], saying [le'mor], "Behold-us [hinnenu]! And-we-will-go-up [ve-alinu] unto [el] the-place [ha-maqom] which [asher] YHWH said [amar], for [ki] we-have-sinned [khatanu]."

> **Key terms:** וַיַּשְׁכִּמוּ בַבֹּקֶר (va-yashkimu va-boqer) = "and they rose early in the morning" — the hiphil of שׁכם (shakham, "to rise early"), showing eager but misguided initiative. רֹאשׁ הָהָר (rosh ha-har) = "the top/head of the mountain." הִנֶּנּוּ (hinnenu) = "behold us" / "here we are" — the presentative particle הִנֵּה with the first person plural suffix, an expression of readiness and availability (cf. Gen 22:1; Isa 6:8). But this readiness comes too late. כִּי חָטָאנוּ (ki khatanu) = "for we have sinned" — they acknowledge sin, but their response (attempting to invade) compounds it with presumption rather than repentance. **LXX:** καὶ ὀρθρίσαντες τὸ πρωὶ ἀνέβησαν ἐπὶ τὴν κορυφὴν τοῦ ὄρους λέγοντες Ἰδοὺ ἡμεῖς ἀναβησόμεθα εἰς τὸν τόπον ὃν εἶπεν κύριος, ὅτι ἡμάρτομεν ("and rising early in the morning they went up to the top of the mountain, saying: behold, we will go up to the place which the Lord said, for we have sinned").

**41** And-said [va-yomer] Moses [Mosheh], "Why [lammah] [is] this [zeh] — you [attem] [are] transgressing [overim] the-mouth of [et-pi] YHWH? And-it [ve-hi] will-not [lo] succeed [titslakh]."

> **Key terms:** עֹבְרִים אֶת־פִּי יְהוָה (overim et pi YHWH) = "transgressing the mouth of YHWH" — the qal participle of עבר (avar, "to cross over, transgress") with the direct object "the mouth/command of YHWH." The same verb that means "to cross over" (into the land) now means "to transgress" (YHWH's command) — a bitter wordplay. וְהִיא לֹא תִצְלָח (ve-hi lo titslakh) = "and it will not succeed/prosper" — the qal imperfect of צלח (tsalakh, "to prosper, succeed"). The pronoun הִיא (hi, "it/she") refers to the plan or attempt. **LXX:** καὶ εἶπεν Μωυσῆς Ἵνα τί ὑμεῖς παραβαίνετε τὸ ῥῆμα κυρίου; οὐκ εὔοδα ἔσται ὑμῖν ("and Moses said: Why are you transgressing the word of the Lord? It shall not be prosperous for you").

**42** "Do-not [al] go-up [ta'alu], for [ki] there-is-no [ein] YHWH in-your-midst [be-qirbekhem], and-you-will-not [ve-lo] be-smitten [tinnagfu] before [lif-nei] your-enemies [oyeveikhem]."

> **Key terms:** אֵין יְהוָה בְּקִרְבְּכֶם (ein YHWH be-qirbekhem) = "YHWH is not in your midst" — the particle of nonexistence אֵין (ein) with the locative phrase. This is the fatal reversal of v. 9 ("YHWH is with us"): now YHWH has withdrawn his presence. וְלֹא תִּנָּגְפוּ (ve-lo tinnagfu) = "and you will not be smitten" — this is best read as a warning: "lest you be smitten" (the waw-consecutive with the negative carries a purpose/result nuance). The niphal of נגף (nagaf, "to strike, smite"). **LXX:** μὴ ἀναβαίνετε· οὐ γάρ ἐστιν κύριος ἐν ὑμῖν, καὶ πεσεῖσθε πρὸ προσώπου τῶν ἐχθρῶν ὑμῶν ("do not go up, for the Lord is not among you, and you will fall before the face of your enemies").

**43** "For [ki] the-Amalekite [ha-Amaleqi] and-the-Canaanite [ve-ha-Kena'ani] [are] there [sham] before-you [lifneikhem], and-you-shall-fall [u-nefaltem] by-the-sword [ba-kherev], for [ki] on-account-of [al ken] you-turned-back [shavtem] from-after [me-akharei] YHWH, and-YHWH will-not [lo] be [yihyeh] with-you [immakhem]."

> **Key terms:** עַל־כֵּן שַׁבְתֶּם מֵאַחֲרֵי יְהוָה (al ken shavtem me-akharei YHWH) = "because you turned back from after YHWH" — the causal conjunction עַל כֵּן (al ken, "therefore, because") introduces the reason YHWH will not be with them: they abandoned him first. וְלֹא־יִהְיֶה יְהוָה עִמָּכֶם (ve-lo yihyeh YHWH immakhem) = "and YHWH will not be with you" — the definitive withdrawal of divine accompaniment. Without YHWH, military action is suicide. **LXX:** ὅτι ὁ Αμαληκ καὶ ὁ Χαναναῖος ἐκεῖ ἔμπροσθεν ὑμῶν, καὶ πεσεῖσθε μαχαίρᾳ, οὗ εἵνεκεν ἀπεστράφητε ἀπειθοῦντες κυρίῳ, καὶ οὐκ ἔσται κύριος ἐν ὑμῖν ("for the Amalekite and the Canaanite [are] there before you, and you shall fall by the sword, because you turned away disobeying the Lord, and the Lord will not be among you").

**44** And-they-acted-presumptuously [va-ya'pilu] to-go-up [la-alot] unto [el] the-top of [rosh] the-mountain [ha-har], but-the-ark of [va-aron] the-covenant of [berit] YHWH and-Moses [u-Mosheh] did-not [lo] move [mashu] from-the-midst of [mi-qerev] the-camp [ha-makhaneh].

> **Key terms:** וַיַּעְפִּלוּ (va-ya'pilu) = "and they acted presumptuously / and they pushed forward recklessly" — the hiphil wayyiqtol of עפל (afal, "to swell, be presumptuous, dare"). This rare verb (cf. Hab 2:4) connotes swollen arrogance, self-willed audacity in defiance of the divine decree. וַאֲרוֹן בְּרִית־יְהוָה וּמֹשֶׁה לֹא־מָשׁוּ מִקֶּרֶב הַמַּחֲנֶה (va-aron berit YHWH u-Mosheh lo mashu mi-qerev ha-makhaneh) = "but the ark of the covenant of YHWH and Moses did not move from the midst of the camp" — the verb מָשׁוּ (mashu) is the qal perfect of מושׁ (mush, "to depart, remove"). The ark — the symbol of YHWH's presence — stays behind. Moses stays behind. They go without God and without their leader. **LXX:** καὶ διαβιασάμενοι ἀνέβησαν ἐπὶ τὴν κορυφὴν τοῦ ὄρους· ἡ δὲ κιβωτὸς τῆς διαθήκης κυρίου καὶ Μωυσῆς οὐκ ἐκινήθησαν ἐκ τῆς παρεμβολῆς ("and forcing themselves they went up to the top of the mountain; but the ark of the covenant of the Lord and Moses did not move from the camp").

**45** And-came-down [va-yered] the-Amalekite [ha-Amaleqi] and-the-Canaanite [ve-ha-Kena'ani] the-ones-dwelling [ha-yoshev] in-the-mountain [ba-har] the-that [ha-hu], and-they-struck-them [va-yakkum] and-they-crushed-them [va-yakkatum] until [ad] the-Hormah [ha-Khormah].

> **Key terms:** וַיַּכּוּם (va-yakkum) = "and they struck them" — the hiphil wayyiqtol of נכה (nakah, "to strike, smite") with the third masculine plural suffix. וַיַּכְּתוּם (va-yakkatum) = "and they crushed/beat them" — the hiphil wayyiqtol of כתת (katat, "to crush, beat in pieces, shatter"), a verb of devastating military defeat. עַד־הַחָרְמָה (ad ha-Khormah) = "until the Hormah" — the place name חָרְמָה (Khormah) derives from חֵרֶם (kherem, "ban, devotion [to destruction]"). The article הַ on the place name is unusual and may suggest the original appellative meaning: "the place of destruction." The same location appears in Numbers 21:3, where Israel later achieves victory and names the place Hormah. Here, in bitter irony, they are the ones destroyed. The chapter that began with the people weeping (v. 1) ends with their military annihilation — the very fate they feared (v. 3) realized through their own presumption rather than through the enemies they were originally sent to face. **LXX:** καὶ κατέβη ὁ Αμαληκ καὶ ὁ Χαναναῖος ὁ ἐγκαθήμενος ἐν τῷ ὄρει ἐκείνῳ, καὶ ἐτρέψαντο αὐτοὺς καὶ κατέκοψαν αὐτοὺς ἕως Ερμαν ("and the Amalekite and the Canaanite dwelling in that mountain came down, and they routed them and cut them down as far as Herman") — the LXX renders Hormah as Ερμαν (Herman).

## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **Verse 1 — LXX expansion:** The LXX adds ὅλην ("whole/all") to "that night" (ὅλην τὴν νύκτα ἐκείνην), intensifying the duration of the weeping beyond what the MT states. The MT simply reads בַּלַּיְלָה הַהוּא ("in that night"). I follow the MT.

2. **Verse 9 — "Their shade has departed" (צִלָּם סָר):** The LXX renders this as ἀφέστηκεν ὁ καιρὸς ἀπ᾽ αὐτῶν ("the time/season has departed from them"), apparently reading צֵל as related to a concept of "time" or "opportunity" rather than "shadow/protection." Some scholars suggest the LXX Vorlage may have read a different word. I follow the MT reading, which yields the stronger metaphor of divine protection removed.

3. **Verse 10 — LXX addition of "in a cloud":** The LXX adds ἐν νεφέλῃ ("in a cloud") to the appearance of YHWH's glory at the Tent of Meeting. This harmonizes with other theophany passages (Exod 16:10; 40:34) but is not present in the MT of this verse. I follow the MT.

4. **Verse 12 — LXX expansion:** The LXX expands "I will make you into a nation" to include "you and your father's house" (σὲ καὶ τὸν οἶκον τοῦ πατρός σου), adding specificity not in the MT.

5. **Verse 18 — Abbreviated creedal formula:** The MT of Numbers 14:18 gives an abbreviated version of the Exodus 34:6–7 self-revelation. The Numbers text omits: רַחוּם וְחַנּוּן (rakhum ve-khannun, "compassionate and gracious"), אֶמֶת (emet, "truth"), and נֹצֵר חֶסֶד לָאֲלָפִים (notser khesed la-alafim, "keeping steadfast-love for thousands"). The LXX of Numbers 14:18 adds καὶ ἀληθινός ("and true"), partially restoring an element the MT omits. I preserve the MT's abbreviated form, which appears to be a deliberate selective quotation by Moses.

6. **Verse 21 — LXX expansion of oath:** The LXX adds "and living is my name" (καὶ ζῶν τὸ ὄνομά μου) to the oath formula. This expansion is not in the MT. I follow the MT.

7. **Verse 22 — "Ten times" vs. "tenth time":** The MT reads זֶה עֶשֶׂר פְּעָמִים (zeh eser pe'amim, "this ten times"), using the cardinal number. The LXX renders τοῦτο δέκατον ("this tenth [time]"), using the ordinal, implying this is the final test in a sequence. I follow the MT's cardinal reading.

8. **Verse 25 — Amalekite and Canaanite placement:** In the MT, this verse states the Amalekite and Canaanite dwell "in the valley" (בָּעֵמֶק). In 14:45, they are described as dwelling "in the mountain" (בָּהָר). Some scholars see a tension or different military encounter; others note that the valley reference in v. 25 is YHWH's statement about the broader territorial situation, while v. 45 describes the specific highland encounter. I translate both as found in the MT without harmonization.

9. **Verse 34 — תְּנוּאָתִי (tenu'ati):** This is a hapax legomenon (occurring only here in the Hebrew Bible). The root is נוא, "to oppose, frustrate, hinder" (cf. Num 32:7). The noun form with the first person suffix yields "my opposition" in the sense of "what it means to have me as your opponent" or "the consequence of opposing me." The LXX interprets as "the fury of my wrath" (τὸν θυμὸν τῆς ὀργῆς μου), and the Vulgate renders ultionem meam ("my vengeance"). I translate literally as "my opposition," preserving the ambiguity.

10. **Verse 44 — וַיַּעְפִּלוּ (va-ya'pilu):** Another rare verb, from עפל, occurring in this form only here and in a different sense in Habakkuk 2:4. The root meaning involves swelling or being inflated, hence "to act presumptuously, to push forward with arrogant boldness." The LXX renders διαβιασάμενοι ("having forced [themselves]"), capturing the sense of willful violence against the divine prohibition.

11. **Verse 45 — Hormah with the article:** The MT reads עַד־הַחָרְמָה (ad ha-Khormah), "until the-Hormah," with the definite article on the place name. This may indicate the name was still felt as a common noun ("the destruction/ban-place") rather than a fully opaque proper noun. The LXX renders it as Ερμαν (Herman), without the article. I transliterate as "the-Hormah" to preserve the article.

12. **Structural note — The chapter's ironic reversals:** The chapter is built on a series of devastating reversals: (a) They wished to die in the wilderness (v. 2); YHWH grants the wish (v. 29). (b) They feared their children would become plunder (v. 3); the children will inherit the land (v. 31). (c) They proposed going back to Egypt (v. 4); they are sent toward the Sea of Reeds (v. 25). (d) They refused to go up when commanded (ch. 13); they insist on going up when forbidden (v. 44). (e) They feared military defeat (v. 3); they suffer military defeat through their own presumption (v. 45). This literary architecture confirms the chapter's careful composition.
